text.title: Nginx Pre-Deployment Checklist
text.summary: Ordered checklist rules for SSL, security headers, rate limiting, compression, and testing before going live.

rule.statement: Run nginx -t before every reload or deploy. Never reload without passing -t — a broken config silently keeps the old config running but the next reload will fail.
rule.rationale: nginx -t validates syntax and checks include paths without restarting. On managed systems, an invalid config on reload can leave the server running stale configuration for hours until noticed.

example.code: sudo nginx -t && sudo nginx -s reload # Or on systemd: sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx

list.item: server_tokens off in http{} block — verify with curl -I and check for Server: nginx/x.x header absence
TLS 1.2 and 1.3 only — verify: openssl s_client -connect host:443 and check Protocol field
All five security headers present — verify with curl -I or securityheaders.com
HSTS max-age at least 31536000 (1 year) for public sites
SSL session tickets disabled (ssl_session_tickets off)
DH parameters file generated if using custom ciphers (openssl dhparam -out /etc/nginx/dhparam.pem 2048)
HTTP to HTTPS redirect: 301 not 302 for permanent redirect
File permissions: config files 644, SSL private keys 600, owned by root
Deny access to .git, .env, and sensitive paths with location ~ /\. { deny all; }

rule.statement: Run SSL Labs test (ssllabs.com/ssltest) for every new domain. Aim for A+ grade. Resolve any warnings before going live.
rule.rationale: SSL Labs checks protocol versions, cipher strength, certificate chain, HSTS, and 40+ other criteria. A+ grade means all current best practices are met and no known vulnerabilities are present.

example.code:
# Quick local SSL check echo | openssl s_client -connect example.com:443 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -dates
# Check certificate chain validity curl -sI https://example.com | grep -E 'HTTP|Strict|X-Frame|X-Content'
# Verify TLS version openssl s_client -connect example.com:443 -tls1 2>&1 | grep -E 'Protocol|Cipher'

list.item: Gzip enabled and returning Content-Encoding: gzip for text responses: curl -H "Accept-Encoding: gzip" -I https://example.com
Static assets served with far-future cache headers (expires max or Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000)
Keepalive working: verify via curl -v --http1.1 and check for Connection: keep-alive in response
Upstream keepalive configured (keepalive 32 in upstream block) to avoid per-request TCP handshakes to backend
worker_processes auto and worker_connections 4096+ in nginx.conf
proxy_pass URL has no trailing slash if location has no trailing slash (avoid double-slash bugs)
X-Forwarded-For set using $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for (not $remote_addr alone)
Proxy timeouts match backend service SLA (proxy_read_timeout, proxy_send_timeout)
Health check endpoint responds correctly through Nginx
WebSocket locations have Upgrade and Connection headers if applicable
Rate limiting applied to auth and sensitive API endpoints
All routes return expected HTTP status codes
HTTP to HTTPS redirect works (301, not redirect loop)
Certificate expiry monitoring configured (alert at 30 days before expiry)
stub_status endpoint accessible from monitoring only, not public
Error log level set to warn (not debug) in production
Log rotation configured (logrotate.d or systemd log rotation)
